ai.title: Create quiz with AI
ai.instruction: Copy the prompt below and paste it into your favorite chatbot (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Copilot...).
ai.importHint: The chatbot will generate an Excel file that you can import directly into SAVINT.
ai.formDescription: Fill in the fields below and the system will automatically create a personalized prompt, ready to copy and paste into your favorite chatbot.
ai.tip: Tip: after generating the Excel file with the chatbot, download it and import it from the My Quizzes page using the "Import Excel" button. SAVINT will validate the file and show you any errors before importing.
ai.prompt:
You are an assistant for creating educational quizzes. You must generate an Excel file (.xlsx) compatible with the SAVINT platform.

The file must contain 6 sheets, one for each question type. Use EXACTLY the sheet names and column names indicated below.

---

📋 SHEET 1: "Scelta Multipla"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Opzione1 | Opzione2 | Opzione3 | Opzione4 | Opzione5 | Opzione6 | Corretta

- From 2 to 6 options per question
- "Corretta" indicates the number of the correct option (e.g. "1" or "1,3" if more than one is correct)
- Time in seconds (default: 30), Points (default: 1000), Confidence: S or N (default: N)

📋 SHEET 2: "Vero o Falso"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Risposta (V/F)

- Answer: "V" for true, "F" for false

📋 SHEET 3: "Risposta Aperta"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Risposta1 | Risposta2 | Risposta3

- Enter the accepted answers (comparison is case-insensitive)
- At least one answer is required

📋 SHEET 4: "Ordinamento"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Elemento1 | Elemento2 | Elemento3 | Elemento4 | Elemento5 | Elemento6

- Enter the elements IN THE CORRECT ORDER (from 2 to 6 elements)
- The platform will shuffle them automatically during the quiz

📋 SHEET 5: "Stima Numerica"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Valore Corretto | Tolleranza | Range Massimo | Unità

- Correct Value: the exact number
- Tolerance: margin within which the score is full
- Maximum Range: beyond this margin the score is zero
- Unit: optional (e.g. "km", "millions", "°C")

📋 SHEET 6: "Abbinamenti"
Columns: Domanda | Tempo (sec) | Punti | Confidenza (S/N) | Sinistra1 | Destra1 | Sinistra2 | Destra2 | Sinistra3 | Destra3 | Sinistra4 | Destra4

- Enter matching pairs side by side (Sinistra1-Destra1 is the first pair, etc.)
- Minimum 2 pairs, maximum 4
- The platform will shuffle the right-side elements during the quiz

---

IMPORTANT RULES:
- The first row of each sheet MUST be the header with the exact column names
- The second row is an example (it will be ignored during import)
- Question data starts from row 3
- The Time, Points and Confidence columns are optional (defaults will be used)
- Empty sheets are ignored, you don't need to fill them all
- Generate the .xlsx file, NOT a .csv file

Now create a quiz on the topic: [TOPIC]
Target audience: [TARGET, e.g. primary school, middle school, high school (first two years), high school (last three years), university, adult education]
Language for questions and answers: [LANGUAGE, e.g. Italian, English, French...]

Adapt the difficulty level, language and complexity of the questions to the indicated target. Generate at least 10 questions distributed across different types. Use a mix of difficulty appropriate to the level. Write ALL questions, answer options and answers in the indicated language.

questionHelp.multipleChoiceDesc: The student chooses one or more answers from the proposed options. You can have 2 to 6 options and more than one can be correct.
questionHelp.multipleChoiceEditor: Click the circle next to each option to mark it as correct.
questionHelp.multipleChoicePlayer: The student sees 4 colored buttons and taps the answer.
questionHelp.multipleChoiceScoring: Correct if they select exactly the right answers. Score based on speed.
questionHelp.multipleChoiceExample: What is the capital of Italy?
questionHelp.trueFalseDesc: The student chooses between True and False. The simplest and fastest question.
questionHelp.trueFalseEditor: Click True or False to set the correct answer.
questionHelp.trueFalsePlayer: The student sees two large buttons: True and False.
questionHelp.trueFalseExample: Earth is the third planet from the Sun.
questionHelp.openAnswerDesc: The student writes the answer freely. You can specify multiple accepted answers (e.g. synonyms).
questionHelp.openAnswerEditor: Add all acceptable variants (e.g. 'Rome', 'rome', 'ROME'). Comparison is case-insensitive.
questionHelp.openAnswerPlayer: The student sees a text field and a Submit button.
questionHelp.openAnswerScoring: Correct if the text matches one of the accepted answers (case-insensitive). Score based on speed.
questionHelp.openAnswerExample: What is Earth's natural satellite called?
questionHelp.orderingDesc: The student must put the elements in the correct order. Useful for sequences, processes, timelines.
questionHelp.orderingEditor: Enter the elements in the correct order. The system will shuffle them automatically for the student.
questionHelp.orderingPlayer: The student sees the elements in random order and uses up/down arrows to reorder them.
questionHelp.orderingScoring: Correct if the final order matches the set order. Score based on speed.
questionHelp.orderingExample: Order the planets from the Sun: Mars, Earth, Venus, Mercury
questionHelp.matchingDesc: The student connects elements from the left column with those from the right column. The right column is shuffled.
questionHelp.matchingEditor: Enter the pairs: the left element will be fixed, the right one will be shuffled.
questionHelp.matchingPlayer: The student taps an element on the left, then the corresponding one on the right to create the match.
questionHelp.matchingScoring: Correct if all matches are right. Score based on speed.
questionHelp.matchingExample: Match each capital to its country
questionHelp.matchingAnswer: Rome → Italy, Paris → France, Berlin → Germany
questionHelp.spotErrorDesc: The student must identify lines that contain errors in text or code. There can be more than one error to find.
questionHelp.spotErrorEditor: Write the lines of text/code. Click the line number to mark it as error (turns red). You can add an optional explanation.
questionHelp.spotErrorPlayer: The student sees numbered lines and taps those they think are wrong. They can select more than one.
questionHelp.spotErrorScoring: Partial score: each error found is worth points, each wrong selection removes points (minimum 0). Speed bonus applied to total.
questionHelp.spotErrorExample: Find the lines with errors in the Python code:
questionHelp.spotErrorAnswer:
Line 1: print("Hello") → OK
Line 2: for i in range(10) → OK
Line 3: x = 5 / 0 → ERROR
Line 4: return x → OK
questionHelp.numericEstimationDesc: The student enters a number. The score depends on how close it is to the correct value.
questionHelp.numericEstimationEditor: Set the correct value, tolerance (full score) and maximum range (zero points). You can add a unit of measure.
questionHelp.numericEstimationPlayer: The student sees a numeric field and the optional unit of measure.
questionHelp.numericEstimationScoring: Full score within tolerance. Decreasing partial score up to maximum range. Zero beyond range.
questionHelp.numericEstimationExample: How many km are there between Rome and Milan?
questionHelp.numericEstimationAnswer:
Correct value: 572 km
Tolerance: ±30 km (full score: 542-602)
Maximum range: ±200 km (zero points beyond 772 or below 372)
questionHelp.imageHotspotDesc: The student taps the correct point on an image. Useful for anatomy, geography, technical diagrams.
questionHelp.imageHotspotEditor: Upload an image, then click to position the hotspot (the correct point). Use the sliders to adjust radius and tolerance.
questionHelp.imageHotspotPlayer: The student sees the image and taps where they think the correct point is.
questionHelp.imageHotspotScoring: Full score if the tap falls within the radius. Partial score in the tolerance zone. Zero outside.
questionHelp.imageHotspotExample: Tap the heart in the anatomical diagram
questionHelp.imageHotspotAnswer:
The correct area is a circle centered on the heart.
Tap inside the circle = full score.
Tap nearby = partial score.
questionHelp.codeCompletionDesc: The student completes a missing line of code. They can answer with multiple choice or by writing the code.
questionHelp.codeCompletionEditor: Write the code block and select the line to hide (by clicking the number). Choose whether the student answers with multiple choice or free text.
questionHelp.codeCompletionPlayer: The student sees the code with a missing line (???) and chooses from options or writes the answer.
questionHelp.codeCompletionScoring: Multiple choice: correct/wrong. Free text: normalized comparison (ignores case and extra spaces). Speed bonus.
questionHelp.codeCompletionExample: Complete the for loop in JavaScript:

hubHome.heroSubtitle: SAVINT is the open-source interactive quiz platform for schools: free, libre and built on sharing. Teachers create quizzes, play them in class and make them available to everyone.
hubHome.searchPlaceholder: Search a quiz by subject, topic…
hubHome.schoolBody: Install SAVINT on your own servers and connect it to savint.it to search, download and publish quizzes. Or try it right now without installing anything.
hubHome.whyOpenBody: Free code (AGPL-3.0), public on GitHub. Anyone can inspect it, improve it and adapt it to their school.
hubHome.whyFreeBody: No subscriptions, ads or data selling. Born for schools, it stays free for everyone.
hubHome.whySchoolBody: Designed for the classroom: many question types, practice mode for individual study and statistics for the teacher.
hubHome.whyShareBody: Every published quiz is an experience other teachers can reuse, clone and improve. Knowledge grows when it circulates.
hubHome.howStep1Body: Browse quizzes made by teachers and try them right in your browser, no sign-up needed.
hubHome.howStep2Body: Start a live game: students join with a PIN from their phones, game-show style.
hubHome.howStep3Body: Create your own quizzes and publish them on the hub: your experience becomes a starting point for other classes.
hubHome.registerIntro: You don't need an account to search, try and play quizzes. Sign up when you want to contribute:
hubHome.registerPublishBody: Share them on the hub with teachers everywhere.
hubHome.registerProfileBody: A page collecting your quizzes, ready to share with colleagues and students.
hubHome.registerSchoolBody: Publish and download quizzes right from your installation's dashboard.
hubHome.footer: SAVINT is free software (AGPL-3.0), written by Cristian Virgili with the help of Claude.

install.title: Install SAVINT at your school
install.subtitle: SAVINT is a self-hostable interactive quiz platform: install it on your own servers, keep student data at your school, and connect to savint.it to share quizzes with other teachers.
install.prop1Body: SAVINT is and always will be free for all schools, with no license fees or subscriptions.
install.prop2Body: The code is public on GitHub: you can study it, modify it, and contribute to the project.
install.prop3Body: Self-hosted means quiz and student data live on your server, not on external clouds.
install.step1Body: All you need is a server (VPS or on-premise) with Docker and Docker Compose installed.
install.step2Body: Use the official compose file: the image is pre-configured and ready to use.
install.step3Body: Point a domain to the server and configure HTTPS with a reverse proxy (e.g. Nginx + Certbot).
install.step4Body: From the instance panel, connect it to savint.it to publish and download quizzes from the community.
install.finalBody: SAVINT is free software (AGPL-3.0): download, install and bring interactive quizzes to your school.

affiliation.pageSubtitle: Fill in the form to request a dedicated installation for your school.
affiliation.successBody: Check your email and click the verification link to complete the request.
affiliation.verifiedNotice: Email confirmed. Your request is pending approval by the SAVINT team.
affiliation.errorGeneric: Error while sending. Please try again.

adminAffiliations.title: Admin console — Affiliations
adminAffiliations.confirmDeleteBody: This deletes the request and, if present, the linked installation with its credentials. It cannot be undone.
adminAffiliations.confirmDeleteNamed: Permanently delete “{name}”? This deletes the school and, if present, the linked installation with its credentials. It cannot be undone.

adminAccounts.title: Admin console — Administrators
adminAccounts.promoteHelp: Enter the email of a user already registered on the hub. They'll get administrator permissions.
adminAccounts.promoteSuccess: Done: the user is now an administrator.
adminAccounts.promoteNotFound: No user registered with this email.
adminAccounts.transferHelp: Move all quizzes published by a teacher to the neutral SAVINT account (useful when a teacher or school leaves). The displayed author becomes “SAVINT”.
adminAccounts.transferConfirmBody: All quizzes published by {email} will move to the SAVINT account. This does not automatically reassign them back.
adminAccounts.transferNotFound: No account registered with this email.
adminAccounts.transferSystemMissing: SAVINT account not configured on the hub.
